source_note: Currency confirmed (2026-07-10): S&P stated directly that the Money Manager Database defaults to USD platform-wide for exported asset values (a local-currency view exists only in the Firm Profile screen, not in exports). These figures are USD, not CAD as previously assumed here. Category mapping and plan-type routing reused the rule set established for the US build one day earlier -- no new categories or plan types appeared in this export, and no new judgement calls were needed. SWF segment ($21.48bn) is Alberta Heritage Savings Trust Fund, cleanly tagged 'Sovereign Wealth Funds' at the company level -- no AA-driven override was needed here, unlike the US where several state permanent funds required cross-referencing against the AA file's plan-type tag to be captured. As with every country, a portion of the 'Government' category was deliberately excluded from all pension segments: Gouvernement du Québec ($107.1bn, general treasury), plus several provincial workers' compensation boards (Quebec's CSST $15.8bn, Alberta WCB $10.4bn, Saskatchewan WCB $1.8bn, New Brunswick WCB $1.5bn) and the Office of the Public Guardian and Trustee ($2.3bn) -- none are staff retirement pools, consistent with the Reserve Banks/state-treasury exclusion logic used elsewhere. 782 institutions tracked, $2,661.6bn tracked across all segments.
scorecard_note: Not yet scored — none of the 12 opportunity-scorecard dimensions have been filled in for any Canada segment. Sign in and use the matrix below to start scoring; see Sources & Methodology for the scoring convention and Overall formula.
allocation_note: Built the same real-bottom-up way as every other country: summed actual plan-level AA lines (5,416 rows) into the six standard buckets, with ambiguous plan types (Investment Pool Funds, Hybrid DB/DC Plan, Other Retirement Fund) ratio-split across each institution's actual segments using the two-pass double-count-avoidance logic. Explicitly-named pools (Sovereign Wealth Fund, Endowment Fund, Foundation Fund Or Investment Pool, Other Tax-exempt Fund) routed directly, same as the US build. Non-pension cash pools (workers' comp funds, nuclear decommissioning funds) excluded from allocation, consistent with their exclusion from segment AUM. Same 5%-of-asset-type materiality threshold for style breakdowns. Coverage ranges 59-105% of company-file AUM across segments; the two smallest DB segments (Tax exempt, Union) have the weakest coverage, immaterial at this scale.
